page.title: Marbella Feria San Bernabé: How to Combine the Casetas With a Daytime Cruise

data.meta_description: Combine Marbella feria San Bernabé casetas with a daytime yacht cruise. Charter from Puerto Banús from €749/2h. Book your boat now.
data.summary: This guide shows how to enjoy Marbella feria San Bernabé by day on a yacht from Puerto Banús and by night at the casetas. It covers boat options, departure logistics, and tips for blending both experiences.
key_facts: Feria San Bernabé runs 11–16 June in Marbella city centre.
Casetas open from lunchtime until late; fairground and live music nightly.
Daytime cruises depart from Puerto Banús, 10 min drive from feria site.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h all-inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h with jet ski included.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% VAT.
Return by 18:00 to shower and head to the casetas by 20:00.

faq.q: What is the Marbella feria San Bernabé?
faq.a: It’s Marbella’s annual summer fair, held 11–16 June, honouring the city’s patron saint. The main site on Avenida del Mar has dozens of casetas (marquee tents), fairground rides, live flamenco, and late-night bars. It’s free to enter, and locals dress in flamenco attire.

faq.q: How far is Puerto Banús from the feria?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is about 6 km west of Marbella city centre — a 10-minute taxi ride or 20-minute drive. Plan for €15–20 by cab. The feria is held near the beach at Avenida del Mar, so it’s easy to combine a cruise and the fair.

faq.q: What time should I book a yacht to still enjoy the feria?
faq.a: Book a half-day charter from 10:00 to 14:00 or 12:00 to 16:00. You’ll be back at Puerto Banús by late afternoon. Shower at your hotel or villa, then head to the casetas around 20:00. That gives you four hours of boat time and the full evening at the feria.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a daytime cruise during the feria?
faq.a: For small groups (up to 9), the Astondoa 40 at €749/2h is a great value. For larger parties up to 12, the Mangusta 80 at €4,719/4h includes a jet ski. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

faq.q: Can I anchor near the feria beach and watch from the water?
faq.a: Yes. The feria is held along Avenida del Mar, right by the beach. You can anchor off Marbella’s main beach and see the fairground lights and hear music from the water. It’s a popular spot for sunset mooring before heading ashore.

faq.q: What’s included in the charter price?
faq.a: All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs. Tier B boats also include a jet ski. Pricing on request for other models.

faq.q: Do I need to book the yacht in advance for feria week?
faq.a: Yes. Feria San Bernabé is one of Marbella’s busiest weeks. Boats book up fast. Reserve at least one week ahead. Payment is via bank transfer or card. WhatsApp us for availability on specific dates.

data.body_html:
Marbella feria San Bernabé is the city’s biggest summer party — six days of casetas, fairground rides, and live music from 11 to 16 June. But you don’t have to spend every hour on dry land. A daytime yacht charter from Puerto Banús lets you escape the midday heat, cruise the Costa del Sol, and still make it back for the evening festivities. Here’s how to combine both without rushing.

Why Add a Yacht to Your Feria San Bernabé Plan?

The casetas open from lunchtime, but the real buzz starts after 20:00. That leaves you a solid window for a morning or early afternoon cruise. The Mediterranean in June averages 24°C water temperature and 28°C air — perfect for swimming, sunbathing, and sipping cava offshore. A boat also gives you a different perspective of Marbella’s coastline, from the Golden Mile to Cabopino.

Plus, anchoring off Marbella’s main beach puts you right opposite the fairground. You can watch the lights come on at dusk and hear the music drifting across the water. It’s a memorable way to start your evening.

Best Boat Options for a Feria Day Cruise

Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, a 10-minute drive from the feria site. For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) start at €749 for two hours, all inclusive. That’s enough time to cruise to Cala del Faro, drop anchor for a swim, and return.

If you want more space and a jet ski, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is available from €4,719 for four hours, with a jet ski included. For larger parties, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) can be booked on request — contact us via WhatsApp for pricing.

All charters include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. No extras. Just bring sunscreen and swimwear.

Suggested Itinerary: Cruise + Casetas

10:00–14:00 — Depart Puerto Banús. Head east along the coast past Marbella Marina, anchor off the feria beach for a swim. Lunch on board (bring your own or order from a beach club). Return by 14:00.

14:00–20:00 — Free time. Shower, rest, or explore Marbella’s old town. The casetas start serving food and drink from midday, so you can grab lunch at the fair if you prefer.

20:00–late — Head to the feria. Wear white and red (the feria colours). Dance sevillanas, try churros, and ride the Ferris wheel. Taxi back to Puerto Banús or your hotel.

Alternative: book a 12:00–16:00 slot if you prefer a later start.

Where to Anchor Near the Feria

The best spot is off Playa de la Venus or Playa del Faro, directly in front of the fairground. Depth is 5–8 metres, sandy bottom. You can drop anchor and swim ashore (the beach is public) or stay on board and watch the action from the water. The sunset view over La Concha mountain is spectacular.

For a quieter swim, head west to Cala Cortés or east to Cabopino — both 15–20 minutes from Puerto Banús.

What to Bring and Wear

Pack a small bag: swimsuit, towel, sunglasses, sunscreen (SPF 50+), a light jacket for the evening, and your phone for photos. The boat provides cold drinks and snacks, but you can bring your own food. Remember cash for the casetas — some stalls don’t accept cards.

For the feria, women often wear flamenco dresses (trajes de gitana), men wear white shirts and red scarves. But casual summer wear is fine too.

Booking Tips for Feria Week

Feria San Bernabé is the busiest week in Marbella. Yachts get booked a month in advance. We recommend reserving at least one week ahead. Payment is by bank transfer or card. WhatsApp us for availability on specific dates and boats.

If you’re flexible, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) and Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) are also popular choices — pricing on request.

For more ideas, see our Marbella boat rental guide.

Frequently asked questions

It’s Marbella’s annual summer fair, held 11–16 June, honouring the city’s patron saint. The main site on Avenida del Mar has dozens of casetas (marquee tents), fairground rides, live flamenco, and late-night bars. It’s free to enter, and locals dress in flamenco attire.

Puerto Banús is about 6 km west of Marbella city centre — a 10-minute taxi ride or 20-minute drive. Plan for €15–20 by cab. The feria is held near the beach at Avenida del Mar, so it’s easy to combine a cruise and the fair.

Book a half-day charter from 10:00 to 14:00 or 12:00 to 16:00. You’ll be back at Puerto Banús by late afternoon. Shower at your hotel or villa, then head to the casetas around 20:00. That gives you four hours of boat time and the full evening at the feria.

For small groups (up to 9), the Astondoa 40 at €749/2h is a great value. For larger parties up to 12, the Mangusta 80 at €4,719/4h includes a jet ski. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

Yes. The feria is held along Avenida del Mar, right by the beach. You can anchor off Marbella’s main beach and see the fairground lights and hear music from the water. It’s a popular spot for sunset mooring before heading ashore.

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs. Tier B boats also include a jet ski. Pricing on request for other models.
